_meta.description: Recommended Claude settings for running Zeref Skills Fleet V2
_meta.note: These are recommended defaults, not hard limits. Adjust per session complexity.

model_preferences.selection_note: Use Sonnet for 90%+ of tasks. Sonnet handles UX, content, dev QA, strategy, and routine skill execution at high quality. Upgrade to Opus only for final executive review, AI systems architecture, or multi-skill orchestration passes requiring maximum reasoning depth.

max_tokens._note: Recommended output token ceiling per task type. Claude may return fewer. These caps prevent context bleed.

temperature._note: Lower temperature = more deterministic. Higher = more creative. Set per task type.

priority_load_order: hot.md — recent session context, decisions, open tasks
skill file(s) selected for current task
registry/zeref-skill-registry.json — only if routing decisions needed
context_window_strategy.hot_md_note: Always read hot.md first. It contains the most recent project state, open decisions, and continuity context. Reading it first prevents re-establishing context that already exists.
context_window_strategy.context_compression_trigger: When context exceeds 60% of window, trigger zeref-system-caveman-compressor before continuing.

skill_routing_mode.description: ZEREFOS classifies the task type and selects the minimum required skill stack automatically. Manual override is allowed at any time by naming the skill explicitly.
skill_routing_mode.stack_rule: 1 lead + 1–3 support + 1 QA gate when needed. Never activate the full fleet.
skill_routing_mode.fallback: If classification is ambiguous, surface the top 2–3 candidate skills and ask the user to confirm before executing.

memory_auto_update.note: After any major decision, strategy, architecture choice, or research insight — recommend saving to the appropriate memory file. Do not claim the file was saved unless a write tool was explicitly used.

triggers: Context length exceeds 60% of model window
Moving between environments (Claude Chat → Code → Cowork → Notion → GitHub)
Session produces decisions, file paths, or risks worth preserving
Long session ending that needs continuity
Context is messy, redundant, or unfocused

free_first_policy.description: Before recommending paid tools, third-party services, or premium connectors — always check if the task can be completed with repo-internal files, local scripts, or Claude's native capabilities. Only escalate to external tools when the free path is genuinely insufficient.
examples: Use zeref-validate.py before buying a linting service
Use shared-token-discipline.md before adding a token-counting middleware
Use zeref-skill-registry.json before integrating an external registry API

connector_discipline.rule: Never claim a connector updated a workspace unless the tool call was explicitly executed and confirmed.
connector_discipline.unavailable_connector_protocol: Produce a copy-paste-ready block for Notion / Linear / GitHub / Google Drive. Label it clearly as 'Copy-Paste Block — paste manually into [tool]'.
